prevalence.value: Low in general population; concentrated in historically exposed worker cohorts
prevalence.source: Occupational lung disease epidemiology syntheses; WHO asbestos-related disease context
prevalence.trend: persistent burden due to long latency; underdiagnosis likely

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; high in affected exposed cohorts
burden.mortalityRate: Moderate in severe fibrosis; also increased malignancy risk related to exposure
burden.source: WHO/IHME occupational risk and chronic respiratory disease context

vulnerablePopulations: communities near industrial asbestos use sites (lower risk)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Exposure history plus interstitial fibrosis on HRCT, restrictive spirometry, and exclusion of other ILDs; pleural plaques support diagnosis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: occupational surveillance varies; limited systematic screening
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (irreversible fibrosis); focus on symptom control and prevention of complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Under-recognized without detailed occupational history; HRCT access uneven
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Smoking cessation, vaccinations, pulmonary rehab, oxygen when needed, manage comorbid COPD; malignancy vigilance as clinically indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, uneven occupational health services, legacy exposures with long latency
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Supportive care feasible; prevention via exposure control has high impact
